displayTitle: Coverage Is Designed, Not Hoped For

beats.narration: Your eyes don't sweep a surface evenly. They jump, and whatever sits between jumps goes unseen. That's why the procedure dictates your scan pattern.

payload.title: COVERAGE BY DESIGN
payload.sub: Inspection patterns turn looking into guaranteed coverage.

beats.narration: Procedures specify the pattern: raster passes across a plate, a numbered grid on large surfaces, circumferential bands around pipe.
Random wandering leaves gaps you never notice. Overlap every pass, like mowing a lawn, and move slowly enough for your eye to resolve detail.

payload.heading: Overlap beats wandering
payload.note: OVERLAP EACH PASS · NO STRIP ESCAPES

beats.narration: While the pattern moves, the envelope holds: eye within 24 inches, viewing angle at least 30 degrees, and minimum light on the area.

chips.t: Minimum lux on the area viewed

beats.narration: Here's the rule: coverage is guaranteed by pattern and overlap, never by confidence. It's designed — not hoped for.

payload.sub: Coverage is guaranteed by design — never by confidence.
